title: 10 best herbal creams for nausea

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with nausea because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, gingerol is absorbed into the skin and can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. The cream also has a cooling effect, which can help to calm the stomach and provide quick relief from nausea. This makes it an effective remedy for individuals experiencing nausea due to various reasons.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach, which is often a cause of nausea.

Shogaols: As a type of phenolic compound, shogaols have been shown to have antiemetic effects, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of stomach acid and improving digestion.

Terpineols: These terpenoids have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and other conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and relieving nausea.
Buds: The buds are used in creams for their concentrated gingerol content, which helps to alleviate nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Infuse zingiber officinale rhizomes in 1 liter of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction and let it cool down to room temperature within 30-40 minutes.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100ml of the cooled decoction and 50ml of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved within 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and thicken for at least 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with nausea because of its unique properties. The cream contains quinine, a natural compound extracted from the Cinchona tree, which has been used for centuries to alleviate nausea and alleviate other symptoms associated with stomach discomfort. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties and has the ability to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing the frequency and severity of nausea episodes. This natural remedy provides relief from nausea without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps with nausea by reducing inflammation and relieving pain, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Quinidine: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea caused by digestive issues.
Rubescens: It is a type of alkaloid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nausea and other symptoms associated with digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids that help alleviate nausea and other digestive issues.
Barks: They are used to extract alkaloids like quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They are used to extract alkaloids like quinine and cinchonine, which help to reduce nausea and other symptoms of digestive disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a reputable supplier on a sunny day.
Extract the bioactive compounds from 100g of dried cinchona officinalis bark in 500ml of water for 2 hours.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 25g of shea butter in a heat-resistant bowl over a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine 50ml of the cinchona officinalis extract with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir until smooth.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into 100ml jars and allow to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with nausea because they contain the essential oil anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. This oil helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to nausea. Additionally, anethole has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, further reducing the sensation of nausea and promoting feelings of relaxation and well-being. This makes Foeniculum vulgare creams a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce nausea.
Anethole: Anethone has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause nausea.
Limonene: Limonene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-emetic properties.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used as they contain a high amount of anethole, which helps to relax the stomach muscles and alleviate nausea.
Roots: Foeniculum vulgare roots are used for their medicinal properties, including the presence of anethole and other compounds that help to soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves on a dry sunny day.
Dry the harvested roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 48 hours.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 30 minutes and then remove from heat to cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with nausea because of its unique ability to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the digestive system. The bioactive compounds present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to calm digestive issues. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy circulation, Ginkgo biloba creams may alleviate nausea and provide relief from digestive discomfort. This natural remedy can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing morning sickness or nausea due to medication.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide has a sedative effect on the brain, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce nausea caused by stress or anxiety.

Terpenoids (specifically 5-Hydroxy-7,8-dihydro-Igistanol and 5-hydroxy-7,8-dihydro-igustanol): These terpenoids have a positive effect on the gut and can help reduce nausea and vomiting by regulating gut contractions and improving digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for making creams due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to extract bilobalide, a compound thought to be responsible for the plant's anti-nausea effects, which is often incorporated into creams for nausea relief.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used to make creams due to their potential ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea, although the evidence is less robust compared to leaves and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Infuse 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 1 part of the infused oil with 1 part of a moisturizing base such as coconut oil or shea butter.
Whip the mixture until it becomes smooth and creamy then transfer it to a clean container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with nausea because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The saponins present in licorice root have been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's anti-spasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with nausea. This can provide quick and effective relief from nausea.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with nausea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate gastrointestinal inflammation and discomfort associated with nausea.
Licoricidin: It helps with nausea by possessing anti-emetic properties, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting by interacting with the nervous system.

Isoliquiritigenin: It helps with nausea by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make creams for nausea relief due to their rich content of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make creams for nausea, as they contain flavonoids and saponins that may help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from nausea.
remedy_recipe: Weigh glycyrrhiza glabra root powder 50g and mix it with distilled water 100ml to create a solution.
Heat the solution at 60-70°c for 20-30 minutes to enhance the root's bioactive properties effectively.
Strain the solution through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities completely.
Add 20g of vegetable glycerin and 10g of beeswax to the solution and heat it at 70-80°c.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams helps with nausea because of their unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly piperine, which is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, as well as protecting the gut from oxidative stress. Additionally, piperine has been shown to enhance the bioavailability of other nutrients, which can help replenish and rebalance the body's natural defenses against nausea. This natural synergy can provide effective relief from nausea.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: It helps with nausea by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating nausea and vomiting.
Vasodilatory terpenoids: They help with nausea by reducing inflammation and promoting vasodilation, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by inflammation and poor blood circulation.

Antioxidant phenolic compounds: They help with nausea by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting caused by inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds, specifically their essential oil, are used to make creams for nausea due to their piperine content, which has anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.

Leaves: The leaves of the Piper nigrum plant are used in creams for nausea relief as they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort.
Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits, which are actually berries, are used in creams for nausea as they contain piperine and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Crush 50 grams of dried piper nigrum berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered piper nigrum with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius then remove from heat and let it cool for 1 hour.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the cooled mixture and melt at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes smooth for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with nausea because they contain the active compound menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Menthol can help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing the sensation of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, menthol's ability to stimulate the brain's trigeminal nerve can also help to alleviate nausea. This makes Mentha x piperita creams a popular natural remedy for people experiencing nausea due to motion sickness, pregnancy, or other digestive issues. Regular use can provide relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent to reduce pain and discomfort associated with nausea.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to relieve digestive discomfort and alleviate nausea.
Menthol: Acts as a local anesthetic and vasodilator to calm the stomach, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol, which helps to relieve nausea and provides a cooling sensation.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain menthone, a compound that contributes to the plant's anti-emetic properties, helping to alleviate nausea.
Roots: Mentha x piperita roots are a good source of limonene, which has been shown to have anti-emetic effects, making them useful in creams for nausea relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers at peak potency and fragrance.
Infuse the harvested m x piperita in 2 cups of hot water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain and combine the infused m x piperita liquid with 1 tablespoon beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax fully dissolves and the mixture reaches 120°f to 140°f.
Pour the m x piperita cream mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with nausea because of its natural sedative properties, derived from the root of the valerian plant. The cream is absorbed through the skin, allowing the valerenic acid and other compounds to interact with the body's nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract. As a result, the cream can help alleviate nausea caused by stress, anxiety, motion sickness, or digestive issues, providing a soothing and calming effect on the body.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to reduce nausea by promoting relaxation and sedation.
Valeranone: Exhibits sedative properties, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing anxiety and promoting calmness.
Isovaleric acid: Has a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the plant's sedative and anti-nausea properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and digestive issues when applied topically in creams.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other active compounds that contribute to their anti-nausea and soothing effects when used in creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried valeriana officinalis roots and leaves from a reputable supplier.
Mix the dried valeriana officinalis with 500g of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at a temperature of 40°c to 50°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the mixture and heat until melted.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum creams helps with nausea because they contain a concentrated form of kava, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, work to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. As a result, the creams can help to ease nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, pregnancy, and chemotherapy. By promoting relaxation and reducing stomach irritation, these creams can provide quick and effective relief from nausea.
remedy_constituents: Kavain: It has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea.
Yasimonin: It possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating nausea and vomiting.

Kawain derivatives (e.g., 7,8-dihydrokavain): They have sedative and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract, leading to relief from nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of kava lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and nausea-relieving properties.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a similar composition of kava lactones as the rhyzomes, making them effective in reducing nausea and inflammation.
Barks: They are used due to their ability to provide a gentle and soothing effect, which can help alleviate nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh p piper methysticum leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried p piper methysticum leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground p piper methysticum powder with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with nausea because they contain the extract of the passionflower plant, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the nervous system. The creams work by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common causes of nausea. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, they can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Passiflora incarnata may also contribute to its nausea-relieving effects, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals experiencing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with nausea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system.

Alkaloids (specifically harmine and harmaline): They help with nausea by interacting with the brain's serotonin receptors, thereby reducing nausea and vomiting associated with chemotherapy, pregnancy, and other conditions.

Phenolic acids (specifically chlorogenic acid): They help with nausea by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical involved in triggering nausea and vomiting, and also by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Fruits: The fruits are used because they are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help ease nausea.
Barks: The barks are used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids that have a sedative effect and can help reduce nausea and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves for extraction usually 50 grams of plant material.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 30 grams of the dried plant material with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for efficient extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and filter the resulting herbal infusion to obtain the final cream base.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for nausea are topical formulations containing plant extracts that help alleviate nausea symptoms by reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for nausea include Zingiber officinale, Cinchona officinalis, Foeniculum vulgare, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Piper nigrum, Mentha x piperita, Valeriana officinalis, Piper methysticum, and Passiflora incarnata. If left untreated, nausea can disrupt daily routines, cause missed work or school, and negatively impact relationships and overall well-being, leading to feelings of frustration and isolation.
